stories.title: D49 Budget Vote Is Tonight at 6:30 PM — What Happens After July 1
stories.angle: District 49's Board of Education votes TONIGHT at 6:30 PM at Sand Creek High School (7005 Carefree Cir N) on the 2026–27 budget. This vote finalizes the approximately $2 million in cuts the district approved in February following a fiscal exigency declaration in January 2026. Impacted programs include reductions in non-core teacher positions, supplemental staffing, Base49 before/after-school care, PEAK programs, preschool, and Elevates 18–21 specialized services. The vote is a public meeting — families can attend and speak during the public comment period. Budget takes effect July 1, less than 3 weeks away. d49.org for the agenda.

x_posts.text:
D49 Board votes on the 2026-27 budget TONIGHT.

Base49, preschool, PEAK, and Elevates 18–21 are all on the line. Budget takes effect July 1.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
📋 The D49 budget vote is happening TONIGHT — and if you have kids in programs like Base49, preschool, or PEAK, this one matters.

The Board of Education votes tonight at 6:30 PM at Sand Creek High School (7005 Carefree Cir N) on the 2026–27 district budget. This vote makes official the roughly $2 million in cuts the board approved in February following the district's fiscal exigency declaration in January.

Programs in the crosshairs: Base49 before/after-school care, PEAK enrichment, preschool seats, Elevates 18–21 specialized services, and reductions in non-core teacher and supplemental staff positions. These cuts take effect July 1 — less than three weeks away.

The meeting is open to the public. There's a public comment period where families can speak directly to the board. If tonight's the night you want to show up, the agenda is at d49.org. Whether you attend or not, knowing what's coming by July 1 helps you plan.

What's already happening in your family because of these D49 budget changes?
facebook.image_caption: Tonight: D49 Board votes on the 2026–27 budget at 6:30 PM, Sand Creek High School. Base49, preschool, PEAK, Elevates 18–21 are all part of the ~$2M in cuts. Budget goes live July 1. Full agenda at d49.org. 📋

stories.title: Flanagan Park Getting $750K Federal Makeover — New Playground, ADA Trail, Basketball Courts
stories.angle: A central Colorado Springs neighborhood park near Nevada Avenue and Fillmore Street is getting a major upgrade this summer. The City of Colorado Springs received a $750,000 federal Community Development Block Grant to rehabilitate Flanagan Park. Planned improvements include a brand-new playground structure, a new ADA-accessible walking trail with lighting, a new basketball court, court resurfacing, and screen fencing. The park, built in the mid-1970s, serves a multigenerational neighborhood including families with young children, older adults, and nearby assisted-living residents. City crews will begin work later this summer, with the project expected to be complete by January 2027. The improved park will be more accessible and safer for families year-round.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Flanagan Park in Colorado Springs is getting a $750K federal renovation.

New playground, ADA-accessible trail with lighting, and basketball court. Work starts this summer.

Near Nevada Ave & Fillmore St.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Flanagan Park is about 50 years old. This summer, a $750K federal grant funds its first major renovation.

Completion: January 2027. More playground space, safer paths, better lighting.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
🛝 Good news for Colorado Springs families: Flanagan Park is getting a major upgrade this summer.

The City of Colorado Springs received a $750,000 federal Community Development Block Grant to rehabilitate Flanagan Park, a neighborhood park near Nevada Avenue and Fillmore Street that was built in the mid-1970s. That's roughly 50 years of wear on equipment, trails, and courts — and this grant finally changes that.

What's coming: a brand-new playground structure, a new ADA-accessible walking trail with lighting, a new basketball court, court resurfacing, and screen fencing. City crews will begin work later this summer, with the full project expected to be complete by January 2027.

The CDBG grant program specifically targets improvements in lower-to-moderate income neighborhoods, meaning federal dollars are going directly to community infrastructure. For the families, older adults, and nearby assisted-living residents who rely on this park, the improvements mean a safer, more accessible space year-round.

Has your family visited Flanagan Park? What improvements are you most looking forward to?
facebook.image_caption: 🛝 Flanagan Park near Nevada Ave & Fillmore St is getting a $750K federal makeover — new playground, ADA trail with lighting, and basketball court. Work starts this summer, done by January 2027. About time for a 50-year-old park.

stories.title: Recall Alert — Wiifo Toddler Tower Stools: 9,700 Units Can Collapse and Entrap Children
stories.angle: The CPSC recalled approximately 9,700 Wiifo-brand Children's Tower Stools (model LT005) on April 23, 2026 due to entrapment and fall hazards. The stools can collapse or tip over while in use, and a child's torso can fit through the side openings — creating a risk of serious injury or death. The firm reported 22 incidents of stools collapsing, resulting in 6 injuries (contusions and scrapes). The stools were sold on Amazon from June 2022 through March 2026 for about $60. They come in white, natural, and light wood finishes with "Model LT005" on the underside label. Remedy: full refund. Stop using immediately, disassemble, take photo, send to support@wiifo.net. IMPORTANT NOTE: Families who own a Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Tower (recalled May 14, 2026, covered June 10) should also check that recall — these are different products but both involve child step towers.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Wiifo Tower Stool (Model LT005) — 9,700 units recalled by CPSC.

Stools collapse and can trap a child's torso. 22 incidents, 6 injuries.

Stop use now. Email support@wiifo.net for full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Wiifo Tower Stool recall (Model LT005) is separate from the Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Tower recall.

If you have either product, stop use. See CPSC.gov for details and refund info.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
⚠️ If you have a Wiifo Children's Tower Stool at home, stop using it now — the CPSC has issued a recall.

The Consumer Product Safety Commission recalled approximately 9,700 Wiifo-brand Children's Tower Stools (Model LT005) on April 23, 2026 due to entrapment and fall hazards. The stools can collapse or tip while a child is using them — and the side openings are large enough that a child's torso can become trapped. That's not a minor risk; it's a serious injury hazard.

The CPSC received 22 reports of stools collapsing, with 6 injuries including contusions and scrapes. These stools were sold exclusively on Amazon between June 2022 and March 2026 for about $60. They come in white, natural, and light wood finishes. Check the underside label for "Model LT005."

If you have one: stop using it, disassemble it, take a photo, and send that photo to support@wiifo.net to claim your full refund. One more thing: this is a different recall from the Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Tower recall issued May 14, 2026. If you acted on the Guidecraft recall, you still need to check for the Wiifo.

Do you have one of these at home? Share this post so other parents can check.
facebook.image_caption: ⚠️ CPSC RECALL — Wiifo Children's Tower Stool (Model LT005): 9,700 units. Stools collapse and can trap a child's torso. 22 incidents, 6 injuries. Sold on Amazon June 2022–March 2026 (~$60). Stop use, disassemble, email support@wiifo.net for full refund. Different from the Guidecraft recall.

The April 23, 2026 date makes this recent. Many families who bought on Amazon June 2022–March 2026 may still have this product. Important to distinguish from the Guidecraft Kitchen Helper Tower recall covered June 10 — both are child step towers, different brands and models.

stories.title: D49 Free Summer Meals End June 19 — 8 Days Left to Use This Free Resource
stories.angle: D49's free summer meal program ends in just 8 days on June 19. Any child age 1–18 can get free breakfast and lunch at D49 sites — no registration, no ID, no income verification required. Springs Ranch Elementary is one confirmed site. Check d49.org for the full site list and hours. This program is separate from District 11's summer meal program, which runs through July 31. With summer in full swing and budgets stretched, this is one of the most accessible free food resources in Colorado Springs — and it's ending soon.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
D49 free summer meals end in 8 days — June 19 is the last day.

Any child ages 1–18 gets free breakfast and lunch. No registration. No ID. No income check.

Find sites at d49.org.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting
x_posts.tweet_type: FOLLOW-UP (first covered June 6; now 8 days to cutoff)

facebook.long_form:
🍽️ Eight days left to use this free resource — and a lot of Colorado Springs families still don't know it exists.

D49's summer meal program ends June 19. That's eight days from today. Any child ages 1 through 18 can walk in and get free breakfast and lunch at D49 sites — no registration, no ID, no income verification required. None. You just show up.

Springs Ranch Elementary is one confirmed site. The full list of sites and hours is at d49.org. Hours vary by location, so it's worth checking the specific site nearest to you.

One important note: this is D49's program, and it ends June 19. District 11's summer meal program is separate and runs through July 31. If your kids are enrolled in D11 or you're in a D11 neighborhood, you have more time — but D49 families don't. Budget-stretching summer meal options are rare, and this one requires zero paperwork.

Did you know about this program? Tag a family who could use it.
facebook.image_caption: 🍽️ D49 free summer meals end in 8 days — June 19 is the cutoff. Any kid ages 1–18, no registration, no ID, no income check needed. Free breakfast and lunch. Full site list at d49.org. Pass this along.

Springs Ranch Elementary is geographically useful for families in the northeast part of COS. With Base49 cuts coming July 1, summer food access becomes even more relevant for budget-stretched families.

stories.title: WMMI Free Family Day TOMORROW — June 12, Blacksmith Demo, Gold Panning, Hayrides
stories.angle: Last reminder: the Western Museum of Mining and Industry (225 North Gate Blvd) holds a free Family Day TOMORROW, Saturday June 12. Watch the historic stamp mill machinery in action, see a live blacksmith demonstration, take a tractor-pulled hayride, and watch the Gold Panning Championships. All outdoor activities are free. The museum is located near the north end of Colorado Springs. Great weather activity for families who want to get outside without spending money. Open during regular museum hours; call 719-488-0880 or visit wmmi.org for details.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Last reminder: WMMI Free Family Day is TOMORROW — Saturday, June 12.

Watch the stamp mill, see a blacksmith, try gold panning, take a hayride. All free.

225 North Gate Blvd · 719-488-0880

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting
x_posts.tweet_type: FOLLOW-UP (covered June 10; today is the last reminder before the event)

facebook.long_form:
⛏️ Tomorrow is WMMI's Free Family Day — and it's one of the more fun free things you can do in Colorado Springs this weekend.

The Western Museum of Mining and Industry (225 North Gate Blvd) holds its Free Family Day this Saturday, June 12. Watch the historic stamp mill machinery in action, see a live blacksmith demonstration, take a tractor-pulled hayride, and catch the Gold Panning Championships. All outdoor activities are free.

This isn't a quiet museum visit — it's hands-on, loud, and genuinely engaging for kids. The gold panning event especially tends to be a crowd-pleaser for kids who've never tried it. The museum is near the north end of Colorado Springs, off I-25 near the Air Force Academy exit.

For questions or hours, call 719-488-0880 or visit wmmi.org. No ticket purchase needed for the outdoor activities.

Have you been to WMMI before with your family? What was the highlight for your kids?
facebook.image_caption: ⛏️ TOMORROW — WMMI Free Family Day, Saturday June 12. Stamp mill demos, live blacksmith, tractor hayride, and Gold Panning Championships. All outdoor activities are FREE. 225 North Gate Blvd, Colorado Springs. Call 719-488-0880 or visit wmmi.org for details.
